keyPoints: Age-stratified cross-cohort RCT design to isolate adolescents (12–17), college students (18–24), and early-career professionals (25–35) effects on AI companionship.
Harmonized, validated measures of loneliness, empathy, and real-life social-skill transfer across cohorts.
Embodiment as a design variable with age moderation to test whether embodied vs disembodied AI differentially affects cohorts.
Longitudinal follow-ups at 3, 6, and 12 months to assess durability and transfer.
Ethical safeguards and parental consent protocols for minors, with safety monitoring and opt-out provisions.
Pre-registration with explicit hypotheses, cohort definitions, and age-by-treatment interaction analyses.
Multi-site recruitment and EMA-rich data collection to enhance generalizability and ecological validity.
